spec_match.rationale: All preconditions enumerated (secure context, IWA scheme, permissions-policy, API surface, Screen Details) are the same ones the spec and chromestatus blink-dev intent list as gates.

interactive.rationale: Probe button enumerates each precondition as a tile (green/red), try-capture button actually invokes the API and logs the rejection reason. Honest debugging surface.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises 'tile-by-tile gate inspection: find the missing precondition fast' and that is exactly the deliverable.

edge_cases.rationale: The permissions-policy probe uses `document.featurePolicy.allowsFeature`, which has been deprecated in favour of `document.permissionsPolicy` / `Permissions-Policy` header. On modern Chrome `featurePolicy` may be undefined → probe always reads false. Real cap on the demo's utility outside the happy path.

references.rationale: See also section only points to sibling concepts — there is NO link to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6284029979525120 or the spec, which violates CLAUDE.md rule #3 (self-heal id embed).

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout, cell.ok / cell.bad both set background + color → no light-on-light regression.

openQuestions.title: Add the missing chromestatus.com reference
openQuestions.detail: Rule #3 of CLAUDE.md says every page must embed https://chromestatus.com/feature/6284029979525120 — currently this page only links to sibling concepts and the slug-recovery script cannot recover the directory if it gets renamed. Add the canonical reference and the WICG spec.

openQuestions.title: Replace deprecated featurePolicy probe
openQuestions.detail: `document.featurePolicy` was deprecated in favour of `document.permissionsPolicy` / `PermissionPolicy` checks via the Permissions Policy interface. On current Chrome this probe will always return false on the regular browser, which is fine, but inside an IWA it will also (incorrectly) return false. Switch to a more reliable check (e.g. fetching the page from an isolated-app scheme and reading `document.featurePolicy` if defined, else falling back to feature detection).

openQuestions.title: Probe MultiScreenCaptureAllowedForUrls policy
openQuestions.detail: The enterprise policy is one of the four gates the page lists, but is not actually probed (you cannot read enterprise policy from JS). Add an explicit cell that says 'cannot be probed from JS — check chrome://policy' so the user knows that gate must be verified out-of-band.

summary: Useful debugging tile-matrix that lays out every gate in plain sight. Missing the chromestatus reference link (a major CLAUDE.md violation) and uses a deprecated featurePolicy API for one probe.
